typecheck.details: 0 tests (no Rust-level unit tests), compilation successful

appliedUpdates.description: Added commands for optimized benchmarks (10mb/100mb/1gb), mixmeta pipeline (10mb/100mb), and convergence analysis (10mb/100mb) to services.yaml for convenience and discoverability

Created .factory/library/analysis-quirks.md documenting: (1) mixmeta qtest() I² manual computation for multivariate models, (2) crossbasis() NA behavior for incomplete lag windows, (3) single-stage vs two-stage CI coverage differences (expected statistical behavior)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The TDD (test-first) approach should be marked as optional or have explicit guidance for data generation and benchmark scripts, where designing the algorithm/DGP first and then writing validation tests is more practical. Multiple workers independently made this deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features run-optimized-benchmarks and generate-truth-data both documented deviations from the TDD step in the analysis-worker skill. Both workers independently identified that writing tests before the script exists is impractical for these script types.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Remove or clarify the 'force R fallback to get baseline timing' language in feature descriptions. The dlnm package architecture uses tryCatch — there's no mechanism to force R fallback via an env var or flag. The pipeline uses whatever backend is available.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature setup-mixmeta-pipeline worker identified this gap. The feature description requested forcing R fallback, but this isn't possible with the current crossbasis()/crosspred() tryCatch dispatch pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note that crossreduce() prints 'centering value unspecified' messages when no cen argument is provided. This is expected behavior and workers should not treat it as an error.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature run-comparison-analysis worker flagged this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges after encountering verbose output during pipeline runs.

rejectedObservations.observation: data.table integer() quirk producing unexpected length-1 vectors
rejectedObservations.reason: Too specific to one code path in run-comparison-analysis. Not reusable knowledge — the workaround (use rep(0L, n) instead of integer(n)) is standard R and doesn't warrant library documentation.

rejectedObservations.observation: RDS format for truth data follows project patterns
rejectedObservations.reason: Already follows convention — no action needed. This is confirmation of existing practice, not a gap.
